mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate transmits through discount-rate relief and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the policy rate is falling or remains near zero.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive on average during periods when FEDFUNDS is flat or declining, or if Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment above a recent trend signals deteriorating household income and risk appetite, which should depress equity prices and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative average 20d SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered cases over a 3-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in retail sales indicates stronger consumer demand and revenue momentum for large-cap firms, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if rising RSAFS is not associated with above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the sign of the return relationship flips in more than two consecutive rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production signals weaker current output and inventory correction, which should lower cyclical earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive-month declines in INDPRO fail to predict negative 20d SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate for negative returns is below 55% in triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is rising while policy rates stay pinned near zero, real rates fall and liquidity conditions loosen, which should support higher S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL is rising year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-plus-low-rate condition does not outperform a single-series CPI or FEDFUNDS rule, or if triggered periods do not show positive average 20d SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate signals improving labor income and consumption capacity, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE combination does not deliver higher 60d forward returns than either series alone, or if the sign is not positive in at least 55% of expanding-labor episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts is an early warning of demand retrenchment and tighter credit transmission, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the housing trend turns sharply down.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if sharp HOUST declines are not followed by weaker 20d SPX returns, or if the triggered negative-return rate is below 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising industrial production together with improving payrolls indicates broad cyclical normalization and supports equity multiples and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint upturn in INDPRO and PAYEMS does not outperform single-variable signals, or if positive 20d SPX returns occur in fewer than 55% of combined-recovery observations.
